speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Thus have I yielded up into your hand

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: The circle of my glory.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Take again

speaker: PANDULPH
text: From this my hand, as holding of the Pope,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Your sovereign greatness and authority.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Now keep your holy word. Go meet the French,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: And from his Holiness use all your power

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: To stop their marches ’fore we are inflamed.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Our discontented counties do revolt,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Our people quarrel with obedience,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Swearing allegiance and the love of soul

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: To stranger blood, to foreign royalty.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: This inundation of mistempered humor

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Rests by you only to be qualified.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Then pause not, for the present time’s so sick

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: That present med’cine must be ministered,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Or overthrow incurable ensues.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: It was my breath that blew this tempest up,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Upon your stubborn usage of the Pope;

speaker: PANDULPH
text: But since you are a gentle convertite,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: My tongue shall hush again this storm of war

speaker: PANDULPH
text: And make fair weather in your blust’ring land.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: On this Ascension Day, remember well:

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Upon your oath of service to the Pope,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Go I to make the French lay down their arms.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Is this Ascension Day? Did not the prophet

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Say that before Ascension Day at noon

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: My crown I should give off? Even so I have.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: I did suppose it should be on constraint,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: But, God be thanked, it is but voluntary.

speaker: BASTARD
text: All Kent hath yielded. Nothing there holds out

speaker: BASTARD
text: But Dover Castle. London hath received

speaker: BASTARD
text: Like a kind host the Dauphin and his powers.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Your nobles will not hear you, but are gone

speaker: BASTARD
text: To offer service to your enemy;

speaker: BASTARD
text: And wild amazement hurries up and down

speaker: BASTARD
text: The little number of your doubtful friends.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Would not my lords return to me again

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: After they heard young Arthur was alive?

speaker: BASTARD
text: They found him dead and cast into the streets,

speaker: BASTARD
text: An empty casket where the jewel of life

speaker: BASTARD
text: By some damned hand was robbed and ta’en away.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: That villain Hubert told me he did live!

speaker: BASTARD
text: So, on my soul, he did, for aught he knew.

speaker: BASTARD
text: But wherefore do you droop? Why look you sad?

speaker: BASTARD
text: Be great in act, as you have been in thought.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Let not the world see fear and sad distrust

speaker: BASTARD
text: Govern the motion of a kingly eye.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Be stirring as the time; be fire with fire;

speaker: BASTARD
text: Threaten the threat’ner, and outface the brow

speaker: BASTARD
text: Of bragging horror. So shall inferior eyes,

speaker: BASTARD
text: That borrow their behaviors from the great,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Grow great by your example and put on

speaker: BASTARD
text: The dauntless spirit of resolution.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Away, and glister like the god of war

speaker: BASTARD
text: When he intendeth to become the field.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Show boldness and aspiring confidence.

speaker: BASTARD
text: What, shall they seek the lion in his den

speaker: BASTARD
text: And fright him there? And make him tremble there?

speaker: BASTARD
text: O, let it not be said! Forage, and run

speaker: BASTARD
text: To meet displeasure farther from the doors,

speaker: BASTARD
text: And grapple with him ere he come so nigh.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: The legate of the Pope hath been with me,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: And I have made a happy peace with him,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: And he hath promised to dismiss the powers

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Led by the Dauphin.

speaker: BASTARD
text: O inglorious league!

speaker: BASTARD
text: Shall we upon the footing of our land

speaker: BASTARD
text: Send fair-play orders and make compromise,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Insinuation, parley, and base truce

speaker: BASTARD
text: To arms invasive? Shall a beardless boy,

speaker: BASTARD
text: A cockered silken wanton, brave our fields

speaker: BASTARD
text: And flesh his spirit in a warlike soil,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Mocking the air with colors idly spread,

speaker: BASTARD
text: And find no check? Let us, my liege, to arms!

speaker: BASTARD
text: Perchance the Cardinal cannot make your peace;

speaker: BASTARD
text: Or if he do, let it at least be said

speaker: BASTARD
text: They saw we had a purpose of defense.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Have thou the ordering of this present time.

speaker: BASTARD
text:
Away, then, with good courage!
                 
                (
                Aside
                .
                )
               Yet I know

speaker: BASTARD
text: Our party may well meet a prouder foe.
